content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "auth-rbac",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 52,
    "domain_depth": 58,
    "data_richness": 40,
    "interaction_quality": 45,
    "ai_integration": 10,
    "mobile_first": 35,
    "performance": 50,
    "typescript_quality": 55,
    "error_handling": 42,
    "realtime": 30,
    "security": 48,
    "differentiation": 25,
    "enterprise_readiness": 40
  },
  "overall_score": 41,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "security",
      "title": "ProtectedRoute dev bypass lacks sufficient safeguards and loading skeleton is incomplete",
      "file": "src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx",
      "description": "The ProtectedRoute component renders an incomplete loading skeleton (JSX is truncated/broken mid-render). The authLoading branch has unclosed JSX tags and missing content area, causing potential render crashes. Also the permission check after auth loading does not handle the case where permLoading is true separately from authLoading, risking a flash of unauthorized content.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx, in the ProtectedRoute component: BEFORE: The authLoading branch renders a skeleton with unclosed JSX (the content area skeleton comment is followed by truncated code). The component also does not check permLoading independently, so after auth resolves but before permissions resolve, children may briefly render. AFTER: 1) Complete the authLoading skeleton to include a proper content area with 3 skeleton rows inside a flex container, properly closing all divs. 2) Add a second loading check: if (!authLoading && permLoading) return the same skeleton layout. 3) After both loading states resolve, check isSessionValid and user: if (!user || !isSessionValid) return <Navigate to='/login' state={{ from: location }} replace />. 4) Then check module permission: if (moduleId && !canAccessModule(moduleId)) return <RequestAccessPage moduleName={moduleName || moduleId} />. 5) If requiredPermission is set and !hasPermission(requiredPermission), return <RequestAccessPage moduleName={moduleName} />. 6) Otherwise render children. Ensure all JSX tags are properly closed. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "PermissionGate RequestAccessPage JSX is truncated with broken render",
      "file": "src/components/auth/PermissionGate.tsx",
      "description": "The RequestAccessPage component's JSX is cut off mid-render (ends at 'return (' with no closing). This will cause a TypeScript compilation error or runtime crash. The component must render a full access-denied page with role display, explanation, and navigation back button.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/auth/PermissionGate.tsx, in the RequestAccessPage component: BEFORE: The component body is truncated after 'return (' — no JSX body or closing brackets exist. This causes a build failure or runtime error. AFTER: Complete the RequestAccessPage component to render: 1) A centered container (max-width 480px, margin auto, padding 48px, text-align center). 2) A ShieldAlert icon (size 48, color colors.textTertiary). 3) An h2 heading: 'Access Restricted'. 4) A paragraph: 'You do not have permission to access {moduleName || \"this module\"}. Your current role is {role || \"unknown\"}.' using colors.textSecondary. 5) A button that calls navigate(-1) with text 'Go Back', styled with backgroundColor colors.primary, color white, padding 10px 24px, borderRadius 8px, border none, cursor pointer, fontSize 14px. 6) Properly close all JSX tags and the component function with proper export. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C1-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "Login form lacks ARIA attributes, focus management, and keyboard support",
      "file": "src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx",
      "description": "The ProtectedRoute skeleton loader and access denied states lack aria-live regions, aria-busy attributes, and proper role annotations. Screen readers cannot communicate loading or denied states. The loading skeleton should have role='status' and aria-busy='true'. The Navigate redirect has no announcement.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx, in the ProtectedRoute component: BEFORE: The loading skeleton div has no ARIA attributes. Screen readers see silent empty content during auth loading. The redirect to /login has no accessible announcement. AFTER: 1) On the outermost div of the auth loading skeleton, add role='status', aria-busy='true', and aria-label='Loading authentication'. 2) On the permission loading skeleton (the new one from issue 001), add the same ARIA attributes with aria-label='Checking permissions'. 3) Add a visually hidden span (position absolute, width 1px, height 1px, overflow hidden) with aria-live='polite' that contains 'Redirecting to login' when the Navigate component would render. 4) On the DevBanner div, add role='alert' so screen readers announce dev mode. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C1-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "ProtectedRoute loading skeleton hardcodes sidebar width and lacks responsive breakpoint",
      "file": "src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx",
      "description": "The auth loading skeleton renders a fixed sidebar placeholder at layout.sidebarWidth using window.innerWidth check. This is not reactive (only checks once at render time, not on resize) and the inline style approach means on mobile the sidebar may flash or layout may break. Should use CSS media query or a responsive hook.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx, in the ProtectedRoute component's authLoading branch: BEFORE: The sidebar placeholder uses `display: window.innerWidth < 768 ? 'none' : 'flex'` which is a static check at render time and does not respond to window resize. Also not SSR-safe. AFTER: 1) Remove the window.innerWidth check. 2) Add a CSS class name approach: wrap the sidebar placeholder in a div with className='auth-skeleton-sidebar' and add a <style> tag (or use the existing theme system) that sets `.auth-skeleton-sidebar { display: flex }` and `@media (max-width: 767px) { .auth-skeleton-sidebar { display: none } }`. 3) Alternatively, use a simple state hook: `const [isMobile, setIsMobile] = useState(window.innerWidth < 768)` with a useEffect that adds a resize listener and cleans up on unmount. Use isMobile to conditionally render the sidebar skeleton. The second approach is preferred for consistency with the rest of the app. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "auth-rbac-C1-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "typescript_quality",
      "title": "projectScope assertProjectAccess makes 3 sequential Supabase calls without error aggregation",
      "file": "src/api/middleware/projectScope.ts",
      "description": "assertProjectAccess calls getUser, then project_members query, then projects query, then organization_members query — 4 sequential awaits. If the project_members query returns data but the projects query fails (network error), the error is not caught gracefully. Also the file appears truncated (ends mid-template-literal in assertProjectBelongsToOrg). These truncations will cause build failures.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/middleware/projectScope.ts, in the assertProjectBelongsToOrg function: BEFORE: The function body is truncated mid-string inside a throw new ApiError( call — the template literal and function closing braces are missing, causing a TypeScript parse error. AFTER: 1) Complete the assertProjectBelongsToOrg function: after the .maybeSingle() call, if (!data) throw new ApiError('Project does not belong to this organization', 403, 'FORBIDDEN'). Close the function with proper braces. 2) Also in assertProjectAccess, wrap the three post-auth queries in a try/catch: if any Supabase query throws a network error, catch it and throw new ApiError('Unable to verify project access. Please try again.', 503, 'SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE') instead of letting raw Supabase errors propagate. 3) Ensure the file ends with proper exports and no truncated code. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore enforces role-based access with server-side gating on every API call and has a mature permission matrix across all modules. Their mobile app properly handles auth state transitions without content flashes. SiteSync's auth layer has structural incompleteness (truncated components, broken JSX) that would fail any security review. The PermissionGate pattern is sound in concept but the implementation is literally unfinished — RequestAccessPage has no body. Fieldwire uses simpler role tiers but their auth flow is bulletproof. SiteSync must reach build-passing, complete auth components before any competitive comparison is meaningful.",
  "summary": "The Authentication & RBAC module has a reasonable architectural foundation — PermissionGate, ProtectedRoute, projectScope middleware, and organizationScope middleware all follow correct patterns. However, the implementation is critically incomplete: at least two components have truncated JSX that would cause build failures (PermissionGate's RequestAccessPage and ProtectedRoute's loading skeleton). The ProtectedRoute does not properly sequence auth loading vs. permission loading, risking unauthorized content flashes. Accessibility is absent across all auth states (no ARIA attributes on loading skeletons or access denied pages). The projectScope middleware has truncated code that prevents compilation. Mobile responsiveness uses a non-reactive window.innerWidth check. The five fixes target: completing the ProtectedRoute with proper loading/permission sequencing, completing the RequestAccessPage render, adding ARIA attributes for screen readers, fixing mobile responsiveness in the skeleton, and completing the truncated projectScope middleware. These are all surgical fixes to existing code — no new features, just making the auth layer actually compile and function correctly."
}
```
